extraction.one_line_summary: TEUNS Dylan attacked on the final Mur de Huy hairpins, dropping defending champion Alaphilippe and five-time winner Valverde who conceded for second.
tactical_patterns: Ineos Grenadiers burned Thomas as domestique on penultimate lap, leaving them tactically empty for finale despite pre-race favoritism.
Kragh Andersen's late attack with Van Sevenant before Mur de Huy base maximized distance from favorites without summit energy waste.
Mas sacrificed positioning on steepest 18-19% hairpins specifically for Valverde rather than conserving for own finish.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: expended leading chase on penultimate lap rather than conserving for own climb, suggesting domestique role prioritization over form
rider_signals.PIDCOCK Tom: negative: dropped early on Côte de Cherave/Ahffe circuit despite defending team presence, confirming unresolved health issues
rider_signals.VLASOV Alexandr: positive: third place represents significant Mur de Huy punch for a rider typically stronger on sustained gradients
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: fourth place as defending champion suggests declining explosiveness on 19% hairpin accelerations where he previously dominated
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: conceding second at age suggests calculated points/placement rather than competitive capitulation given Mas's leadout sacrifice
